Customer Objective: Create clear business ownership for AI in this customer environment.
Detailed Requirement: Name a customer-side executive sponsor who approves scope, target roles, success criteria, and broad rollout decisions.
Cadence: At initial rollout and annual renewal
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Microsoft 365 admin center for pilot scope visibility; SharePoint list for decision log
Evidence of Completion: Signed project charter or decision log naming sponsor.
Notes / Guardrails: No broad rollout without a sponsor who can make tradeoff decisions.

Customer Objective: Tie AI use to measurable work outcomes, not generic productivity language.
Detailed Requirement: Document 1 to 3 target outcomes for each pilot persona, including baseline effort, quality issue, or throughput metric.
Cadence: At initial rollout and before each new department expansion
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Power BI for KPI baseline visualization; Excel/SharePoint for outcome register
Evidence of Completion: Baseline-and-target matrix by persona.
Notes / Guardrails: Use role-specific outcomes such as ticket triage speed, proposal drafting time, or meeting recap accuracy.

Customer Objective: Constrain rollout to a deliberate set of users in the customer environment.
Detailed Requirement: Identify the initial pilot group, their job functions, support path, and go/no-go criteria for expansion.
Evidence of Completion: Pilot roster, group export, and rollout criteria.
Notes / Guardrails: Do not start with all users.

Customer Objective: Set user expectations before AI is introduced.
Detailed Requirement: Publish a plain-language launch message covering what is enabled, what is not, where to get help, and what success looks like.
Evidence of Completion: Published communication and acknowledgement path.
Notes / Guardrails: This should be customer-facing, not MSP-internal.

Customer Objective: Keep business, IT, and security aligned after launch.
Detailed Requirement: Run a recurring customer AI steering meeting to review adoption, risk, use-case backlog, and policy decisions.
Cadence: Monthly or quarterly depending on customer size
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Teams meetings; Loop/SharePoint for decision log
Evidence of Completion: Recurring agenda, attendees, and decision log.
Notes / Guardrails: This is a customer governance forum, not an MSP operations meeting.

Customer Objective: Sequence AI work by value and risk in the customer environment.
Detailed Requirement: Maintain a living backlog of approved use cases with owner, business value, data sensitivity, and readiness status.
Evidence of Completion: Approved backlog with prioritization fields.
Notes / Guardrails: Use this to decide which departments expand next.

Customer Objective: Avoid uncontrolled spend as adoption expands.
Detailed Requirement: Define who receives paid AI licenses, which users remain on free/limited experiences, and what budget guardrails apply.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Microsoft 365 admin center usage and license reports
Evidence of Completion: License allocation plan and quarterly review note.
Notes / Guardrails: Budget planning belongs at the customer level.

Customer Objective: Require evidence before moving from pilot to broader departments.
Detailed Requirement: Document expansion gates such as training completion, KPI improvement, support stability, and data readiness.
Evidence of Completion: Signed stage-gate checklist.
Notes / Guardrails: Expansion should be evidence-based.

Customer Objective: Give the customer a sequenced roadmap across departments and maturity levels.
Detailed Requirement: Maintain a 12-month roadmap that shows pilots, hardening milestones, agentic use cases, and dependent cleanup work.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Planner / Project for the web / Power BI
Evidence of Completion: Roadmap with owners and target quarters.
Notes / Guardrails: Useful once the environment moves beyond one or two pilots.

Customer Objective: Clarify what the customer will and will not automate.
Detailed Requirement: Document the customer’s position on acceptable autonomy, regulated data use, external AI services, and customer-facing automation.
Cadence: Annual and upon major policy change
Evidence of Completion: Approved AI risk appetite statement.
Notes / Guardrails: This becomes the anchor for later agentic decisions.

Customer Objective: Set minimum rules for day-to-day AI use by customer employees.
Detailed Requirement: Publish an AI acceptable use policy covering approved tools, prohibited data, human review expectations, and reporting of harmful output.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: SharePoint policy library; Purview communication/compliance references
Evidence of Completion: Versioned policy and acknowledgement record.
Notes / Guardrails: Keep it short enough that employees will actually read it.

Customer Objective: Give the customer a clean list of what is allowed.
Detailed Requirement: Maintain a list of approved AI tools, copilots, chatbots, automations, and agent platforms that employees may use.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: SharePoint list; Defender exposure inventory where available
Evidence of Completion: Approved tools register with owner and purpose.
Notes / Guardrails: Start with approved tools before building a long blocked list.

Customer Objective: Reduce accidental misuse of sensitive data.
Detailed Requirement: Document which data categories may never be pasted, uploaded, or shared into AI tools without formal exception.
Cadence: Annual review and upon regulatory change
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Purview information protection reference; SharePoint policy page
Evidence of Completion: Published prohibited-data list with examples.
Notes / Guardrails: Examples should include customer confidential, regulated data, legal holds, and trade secrets as applicable.

Customer Objective: Require employees to verify important AI outputs before use.
Detailed Requirement: Publish a standard that defines when employees must fact-check, cite, peer review, or manager review AI-generated work.
Evidence of Completion: Published standard and example scenarios.
Notes / Guardrails: Especially important for customer-facing, legal, financial, and security content.

Customer Objective: Prevent uncontrolled enablement of new AI features in the customer tenant.
Detailed Requirement: Require review and approval before turning on new AI capabilities, connectors, browser extensions, or agents in production.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: ServiceNow/Planner paired with Microsoft 365 admin change evidence
Evidence of Completion: Approved change record with rollback notes.
Notes / Guardrails: Apply the same rigor as other production changes.

Customer Objective: Control data handling risk for non-native AI tools.
Detailed Requirement: Perform security, privacy, retention, and supportability review before approving any third-party AI service, plugin, or connector.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Defender for Cloud Apps app governance references; SharePoint checklist
Evidence of Completion: Completed due diligence checklist and approval decision.
Notes / Guardrails: This applies to browser AI, SaaS copilots, and embedded AI add-ons.

Customer Objective: Stop one-off approvals from becoming permanent drift.
Detailed Requirement: Track AI policy exceptions with business justification, compensating controls, owner, approver, and expiry date.
Evidence of Completion: Active exception register with no expired open items.
Notes / Guardrails: If an exception has no expiry, it is not an exception.

Customer Objective: Ensure agents and automations are approved like applications, not side projects.
Detailed Requirement: Require a documented approval path before a custom agent or workflow is published to employees.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Copilot Studio admin controls; Power Platform admin center
Evidence of Completion: Approval record, owner, purpose, and support designation.
Notes / Guardrails: Treat agents like deployable software with business impact.

Customer Objective: Enforce policy by limiting employee access to unsanctioned AI services where business risk warrants it.
Detailed Requirement: Use technical controls to restrict unapproved public GenAI sites, desktop apps, and browser extensions unless explicitly approved.
Cadence: Quarterly review and upon major new AI service adoption
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint / Defender for Cloud Apps / Defender for Web controls
Generic Tooling Category: DNS filtering tools / SSE / CASB / secure web gateway / browser control
Evidence of Completion: Policy-backed block list and control validation test.
Notes / Guardrails: Use least-disruptive controls first, but do not rely on policy alone for high-risk environments.

Safeguard Title: Policy mapping to enterprise risk framework
Customer Objective: Align AI governance to the customer’s broader compliance and risk program.
Detailed Requirement: Map AI safeguards to the customer’s security, privacy, audit, and legal control framework and track gaps.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: SharePoint controlled register; Purview evidence exports where relevant
Evidence of Completion: Control mapping matrix and remediation actions.
Notes / Guardrails: Useful for SOX, HIPAA, PCI, ISO, or contract-heavy customers.

Customer Objective: Reduce account compromise risk before AI is enabled.
Detailed Requirement: Confirm SSO, MFA, and basic identity hygiene for all in-scope users before granting AI access.
Cadence: Before initial rollout and for each new user cohort
Evidence of Completion: Identity readiness checklist and policy evidence.
Notes / Guardrails: AI access should not outrun identity maturity.

Customer Objective: Ensure employees are using supported apps, browsers, and managed devices where required.
Detailed Requirement: Validate supported OS, browser, Office app version, and required update channel for targeted AI experiences.
Evidence of Completion: Readiness report or device/app compliance export.
Notes / Guardrails: This is a customer environment requirement, not an MSP-only concern.

Customer Objective: Prevent failed rollouts caused by blocked services or unstable routes.
Detailed Requirement: Verify required service endpoints, proxies, browser policies, and relevant API reachability for in-scope AI tools.
Cadence: Per rollout wave and after major network changes
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Microsoft connectivity and service health references
Evidence of Completion: Connectivity checklist and test results.
Notes / Guardrails: Particularly important for VDI, proxy-heavy, or locked-down environments.

Customer Objective: Make sure the customer can stop or limit rollout safely.
Detailed Requirement: Document how AI access, licenses, integrations, and user targeting will be paused or rolled back if risk emerges.
Evidence of Completion: Documented rollback plan and owner.
Notes / Guardrails: If you cannot reverse it, you do not fully control it.

Customer Objective: Limit higher-trust AI access to compliant devices when appropriate.
Detailed Requirement: Require compliant or managed devices for sensitive AI use cases, especially where business data can be surfaced broadly.
Evidence of Completion: Policy evidence and test from non-compliant device.
Notes / Guardrails: Use stronger controls for finance, HR, legal, and executive populations.

Customer Objective: Ensure rollout does not depend on broad standing admin rights in the customer tenant.
Detailed Requirement: Review delegated, privileged, and service accounts involved in AI administration and reduce access to least privilege.
Evidence of Completion: Access review output and remediation log.
Notes / Guardrails: Separate daily admin from privileged activation where possible.

Customer Objective: Enable the telemetry needed to prove usage, changes, and incidents.
Detailed Requirement: Turn on required audit, sign-in, admin change, and relevant application logs for AI-related services.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Purview Audit; Entra sign-in logs; Microsoft 365 audit surfaces
Evidence of Completion: Successful test retrieval for an AI-related event.
Notes / Guardrails: Observability depends on this prerequisite.

Customer Objective: Keep experimentation out of the employee production path.
Detailed Requirement: Use a distinct test path, group, or environment for new AI prompts, connectors, policies, or custom agents before production release.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Copilot Studio / Power Platform environments / Entra groups
Evidence of Completion: Documented promotion path and test evidence.
Notes / Guardrails: Even if the platform is limited, a separate pilot group still counts as separation.

Customer Objective: Require time-bound elevation and approvals for sensitive AI administration.
Detailed Requirement: Use time-bound privileged access, justification, and logging for sensitive AI-related admin changes.
Evidence of Completion: Activation logs and privileged role review.
Notes / Guardrails: Advanced hardening for larger customers or sensitive environments.

Customer Objective: Protect API keys, service identities, and certificates used by AI integrations.
Detailed Requirement: Inventory and manage all secrets, service accounts, API keys, and certificates used by AI tools and automations, with rotation rules.
Evidence of Completion: Secret inventory, ownership, and rotation evidence.
Notes / Guardrails: Be especially careful with unattended automations and service accounts.

Customer Objective: Target AI where the customer actually has repetitive work.
Detailed Requirement: Identify the highest-value workflows in the customer environment where AI may improve speed, consistency, or quality.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Excel / SharePoint / Power BI for scoring
Evidence of Completion: Workflow inventory with owner and value score.
Notes / Guardrails: Pick the workflows before picking more tools.

Customer Objective: Preserve human accountability where it matters most.
Detailed Requirement: Define which workflow outputs require human approval before they reach customers, employees, systems of record, or regulated records.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: SharePoint SOPs; Teams approvals where used
Evidence of Completion: Published review matrix by workflow.
Notes / Guardrails: This is one of the most important universal controls.

Customer Objective: Make early AI use cases consistent and teachable.
Detailed Requirement: Create step-by-step SOPs for each approved pilot workflow, including when AI may be used and what manual checks remain required.
Cadence: Per workflow at launch, then quarterly
Evidence of Completion: Published SOP with version history.
Notes / Guardrails: Avoid free-form experimentation in critical workflows.

Customer Objective: Give employees a safe starting point for common tasks.
Detailed Requirement: Publish starter prompts, templates, and examples for approved use cases in the customer environment.
Evidence of Completion: Published prompt library with examples.
Notes / Guardrails: The goal is consistency, not prompt perfection.

Customer Objective: Prove whether AI is helping the customer’s actual work.
Detailed Requirement: Measure baseline time, volume, quality, or error rates for approved workflows before and after AI introduction.
Evidence of Completion: Baseline and trend report.
Notes / Guardrails: No baseline means no credible value story.

Safeguard Title: Escalation and exception path by workflow
Customer Objective: Give employees a clear path when AI output is wrong or risky.
Detailed Requirement: Document workflow-specific escalation points for unsafe output, missing data, misclassification, or bad recommendations.
Evidence of Completion: Published escalation path in workflow SOP.
Notes / Guardrails: Make this visible where users work.

Customer Objective: Respond quickly when AI causes harm, oversharing, or operational disruption.
Detailed Requirement: Define how the customer triages, contains, communicates, and documents AI-related incidents.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Purview audit references; Teams incident collaboration
Evidence of Completion: Approved playbook and exercise record.
Notes / Guardrails: Treat harmful output and oversharing as supportable events, not one-offs.

Customer Objective: Prevent hidden process drift as AI use expands.
Detailed Requirement: Require workflow owners to approve meaningful changes to AI-assisted SOPs, automations, and decision boundaries.
Evidence of Completion: Approved SOP version and change record.
Notes / Guardrails: A prompt change can be a process change.

Customer Objective: Define where AI may draft, recommend, decide, or act.
Detailed Requirement: Create a matrix that explicitly defines allowed automation depth for each workflow and what remains human-only.
Evidence of Completion: Approved autonomy matrix.
Notes / Guardrails: This is critical before agentic rollout.

Customer Objective: Track hallucination, rework, and exception rates by process.
Detailed Requirement: Create a quality scorecard per major AI-assisted workflow and review it with workflow owners.
Evidence of Completion: Scorecard with corrective actions.
Notes / Guardrails: Quality needs to be measured, not assumed.

Customer Objective: Define what business data AI may touch at launch.
Detailed Requirement: Document which repositories, sites, folders, knowledge bases, or applications are approved for AI use in the initial rollout.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: SharePoint list; Purview data map references
Evidence of Completion: Approved data-in-scope register.
Notes / Guardrails: Simple is fine: allowed, limited, or out-of-scope.

Customer Objective: Give employees a usable language for data sensitivity.
Detailed Requirement: Define and publish a simple classification taxonomy, such as Public, Internal, Confidential, and Restricted, with examples.
Evidence of Completion: Published classification standard.
Notes / Guardrails: If the taxonomy is too complex, it will not be used.

Customer Objective: Reduce obvious oversharing before AI is introduced.
Detailed Requirement: Review high-value repositories for broad links, stale permissions, legacy shared drives, and overly permissive groups.
Evidence of Completion: Remediation tracker and before/after review.
Notes / Guardrails: Start with the content AI is most likely to surface.

Customer Objective: Reduce noisy, stale, or duplicated content that weakens AI output quality.
Detailed Requirement: Archive or label stale content, duplicate knowledge, and obvious non-business repositories before broad AI rollout.
Evidence of Completion: Repository cleanup checklist and owner attestation.
Notes / Guardrails: Bad content quality becomes bad AI answers.

Customer Objective: Move from taxonomy to applied labels or equivalent metadata.
Detailed Requirement: Apply sensitivity labels or equivalent metadata to important repositories and content so AI boundaries reflect data sensitivity.
Evidence of Completion: Label policy evidence and test results.
Notes / Guardrails: Start with highest-value repositories if full rollout is too large.

Customer Objective: Reduce the chance that employees move sensitive data into the wrong AI channels.
Detailed Requirement: Deploy controls that detect or block prohibited uploads, prompts, sharing, or outbound movement of sensitive content.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Microsoft Purview DLP / Defender for Cloud Apps
Generic Tooling Category: DLP / CASB / email and web controls
Evidence of Completion: Policy evidence, alerts, and tuning notes.
Notes / Guardrails: Protect data movement, not just data at rest.

Customer Objective: Tighten boundaries where guests or external users intersect with AI-relevant repositories.
Detailed Requirement: Review guest access, shared links, external collaboration settings, and cross-tenant shares for AI-exposed repositories.
Evidence of Completion: Review output and remediation plan.
Notes / Guardrails: AI can amplify whatever the repository already exposes.

Customer Objective: Apply the customer’s recordkeeping rules to AI-generated or AI-associated content.
Detailed Requirement: Define how AI-generated outputs, conversations, or work products are retained, deleted, or placed on hold where applicable.
Evidence of Completion: Retention policy definition and test case.
Notes / Guardrails: Not every AI interaction is a record, but some outputs clearly are.

Customer Objective: Apply stronger rules for PHI, PCI, legal privilege, HR, or other highly sensitive datasets.
Detailed Requirement: Define specific handling rules, approvals, and technical restrictions for regulated or highly sensitive datasets in AI workflows.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Purview advanced labeling / DLP / access policies
Generic Tooling Category: Sensitive data governance / DSPM / data access controls
Evidence of Completion: Approved regulated-data matrix and control tests.
Notes / Guardrails: Do not treat regulated data like ordinary internal content.

Customer Objective: Keep finding new data exposure risks after launch.
Detailed Requirement: Monitor for newly overshared sites, broad links, external sharing, or data sprawl that could affect AI retrieval.
Cadence: Monthly or quarterly depending on change rate
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Purview DSPM for AI / SharePoint governance signals
Evidence of Completion: Recurring risk report and remediation tracker.
Notes / Guardrails: This becomes more important as adoption and content volume grow.

Customer Objective: See who is enabled and whether the customer is actually using AI.
Detailed Requirement: Report enabled users, active users, basic usage trends, and adoption by target department or cohort.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Microsoft 365 usage reports / Viva Insights where applicable
Evidence of Completion: Monthly adoption report.
Notes / Guardrails: This is the baseline service health view.

Customer Objective: Find unsanctioned AI behavior in the customer environment.
Detailed Requirement: Establish a baseline review for unsanctioned AI websites, browser extensions, desktop tools, or file movement patterns related to AI use.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Defender for Cloud Apps / Defender for Endpoint visibility
Evidence of Completion: Shadow AI findings log with owner.
Notes / Guardrails: Visibility must come before enforcement.

Customer Objective: Be able to answer “who did what?” questions when AI use is questioned.
Detailed Requirement: Test that the customer can retrieve relevant audit evidence for AI enablement changes, access, and notable usage events where supported.
Evidence of Completion: Successful test retrieval and documented path.
Notes / Guardrails: If you cannot retrieve evidence, investigations will fail.

Customer Objective: Check early whether AI output is useful and safe in practice.
Detailed Requirement: Sample a small number of outputs from pilot workflows and rate quality, factuality, and required rework.
Evidence of Completion: Sampling log and score summary.
Notes / Guardrails: Observability should include value and quality, not just security.

Customer Objective: Detect likely misuse, policy overrides, or abnormal AI behavior faster.
Detailed Requirement: Create alerts or reviews for suspicious AI-related activities such as blocked uploads, unusual tool usage, or high-risk data access patterns.
Evidence of Completion: Alert catalog, owner, and triage SOP.
Notes / Guardrails: Start with a small set of high-signal alerts.

Customer Objective: Review adoption, spend, and business value together.
Detailed Requirement: Track license assignment, active usage, key workflow KPIs, support burden, and notable risk events in one scorecard.
Evidence of Completion: QBR scorecard with actions.
Notes / Guardrails: This helps stop shelfware and uncontrolled expansion.

Customer Objective: Maintain visibility into what AI capabilities are actually active for employees.
Detailed Requirement: Inventory active copilots, assistants, custom agents, enabled connectors, and key policy states in the customer environment.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Copilot Studio / M365 admin references / SharePoint register
Evidence of Completion: Current inventory with owner and status.
Notes / Guardrails: You cannot govern what you have not inventoried.

Customer Objective: Catch when controls change after launch.
Detailed Requirement: Review whether key AI settings, connector states, browser controls, and access restrictions remain aligned to approved baseline.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Defender / Entra / Purview / admin center exports
Evidence of Completion: Drift review and remediation actions.
Notes / Guardrails: Important once multiple admins and departments are involved.

Customer Objective: Correlate AI usage with data movement, endpoint, and identity signals for stronger investigations.
Detailed Requirement: Link AI-related observations to endpoint, identity, and data security events to understand context and risk.
Evidence of Completion: Correlated investigation workflow and evidence examples.
Notes / Guardrails: Advanced, but valuable for higher-risk customers.

Customer Objective: Look for meaningful deviations in AI behavior over time.
Detailed Requirement: Use analytics to flag unusual spikes in AI usage, unusual departments, abnormal data interactions, or new unsanctioned tools.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Sentinel analytics / Power BI anomaly views
Evidence of Completion: Documented anomaly rules and review output.
Notes / Guardrails: This is an advanced overlay, not a starting point.

Customer Objective: Deploy to a trained customer cohort before broad release.
Detailed Requirement: Enable AI only for a named pilot group that has completed starter training and knows the support path.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Entra groups; Viva Learning / Microsoft 365 training resources
Evidence of Completion: Pilot roster and training completion list.
Notes / Guardrails: Pilot quality matters more than pilot size.

Customer Objective: Use staged rollout waves inside the customer environment.
Detailed Requirement: Create rollout rings such as IT/champions, pilot department, and broader departments with explicit expansion criteria.
Evidence of Completion: Ring plan and targeted groups.
Notes / Guardrails: This makes rollback and support more manageable.

Customer Objective: Match enablement to what employees actually do.
Detailed Requirement: Provide role-based training that covers approved use cases, limitations, prohibited data, and escalation paths for each employee cohort.
Cadence: At launch for existing staff, then per new employee and annual refresh
Evidence of Completion: Training curriculum and completion record.
Notes / Guardrails: Training should be tied to role, not only to the product brand.

Customer Objective: Make sure employees know how AI issues are handled.
Detailed Requirement: Publish a support runbook that defines where employees report AI issues, how incidents are categorized, and expected triage path.
Evidence of Completion: Published runbook and support category.
Notes / Guardrails: Treat AI issues as supportable events.

Customer Objective: Control which systems AI can reach in the customer environment.
Detailed Requirement: Require approval before enabling connectors, plugins, actions, or external data paths that expand AI access.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Copilot Studio / Power Platform admin / M365 admin controls
Evidence of Completion: Connector approval record and inventory update.
Notes / Guardrails: Data path expansion is often the biggest real risk increase.

Customer Objective: Prevent unfinished prompts, agents, or automations from reaching employees.
Detailed Requirement: Use a promotion path for custom prompts, automations, and agents so production changes are tested before release.
Evidence of Completion: Release checklist, tester signoff, and promotion record.
Notes / Guardrails: A separate pilot ring is the minimum if full environment separation is not possible.

Customer Objective: Expand deployment only when the next employee cohort is ready.
Detailed Requirement: Require that each new role or department has completed training, accepted policy, and met data-readiness prerequisites before enablement.
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Entra groups; Viva Learning; SharePoint checklist
Evidence of Completion: Signed enablement gate or checklist.
Notes / Guardrails: Rollout should follow readiness, not just license availability.

Customer Objective: Test the customer’s ability to pause or reverse a rollout.
Detailed Requirement: Run a rehearsal that confirms groups, licenses, access paths, and support comms can be rolled back cleanly if needed.
Cadence: Before major expansion and annual refresh
Evidence of Completion: Rollback rehearsal record.
Notes / Guardrails: If rollback is never tested, it is partly theoretical.

Customer Objective: Track every production agent or custom AI workflow over time.
Detailed Requirement: Maintain a lifecycle register for each production agent or automation including owner, purpose, data scope, review date, and retirement path.
Evidence of Completion: Current lifecycle register with no unknown owners.
Notes / Guardrails: The lifecycle needs to include retirement, not just launch.

Customer Objective: Remove AI access and related exceptions cleanly when employees leave or change roles.
Detailed Requirement: Ensure user offboarding removes AI licenses, agent access, custom exceptions, shared secrets, and elevated roles tied to AI capabilities.
Cadence: Per departing employee or role change
Microsoft Tool Recommendation: Entra ID; M365 admin center; Copilot Studio access review
Evidence of Completion: Offboarding checklist with completed removals.
Notes / Guardrails: This is a customer environment lifecycle step, not an MSP back-office task.
